items.title: Nightly fraud model retrain
items.scenario: A bank trains a gradient-boosted classifier nightly on the past 90 days of labeled transactions (fraud / legitimate). Training runs on a Spark cluster for two hours, updating model weights from (features, label) pairs. During the day, the frozen model scores each live payment in under 50 ms — inference only, no labels at checkout.
items.explanation: Classic train-once (periodically), infer-many pattern: expensive labeled training offline; cheap unlabeled inference at high volume.

items.title: Image classifier API
items.scenario: A team trains a ResNet on 1M labeled product photos using SGD on GPUs for a week. They export the checkpoint and deploy it behind a REST API. Customers upload images (x); the server runs forward pass and returns category ŷ. Upload payloads never include ground-truth labels.
items.explanation: Training consumed labels and updated θ; the serving endpoint is pure inference on new x.

items.title: House price estimator in a CRM
items.scenario: A regression model is fit on historical listings with sale prices. Realtors enter sq ft and zip code for a new listing; the CRM calls predict() and shows an estimated price. The listing has no known sale price yet — inference without y.
items.explanation: fit() learned from labeled past sales; predict() applies the learned mapping when the true price is unknown.

items.title: LLM instruction fine-tuning then chat serving
items.scenario: Engineers fine-tune a language model on (prompt, ideal response) pairs — supervised training with cross-entropy loss over tokens. After training, the chat product runs inference: given a user prompt x, the model generates tokens autoregressively with frozen weights. User messages are not accompanied by reference answers at serving time.
items.explanation: Gen AI still separates a labeled training phase from unlabeled inference at scale.

items.title: Batch scoring vs online inference
items.scenario: An insurer trains a churn model on labeled customer histories. Monthly batch jobs score 10M accounts overnight (batch inference). A separate webhook scores individual signups in real time (online inference). Neither scoring path updates model parameters or requires labels for the accounts being scored.
items.explanation: Inference can be batch or streaming, but in all cases θ stays fixed and labels are not needed for inputs being scored.
